candidates.name: AI-Agent Solo Venture Launch
candidates.summary: An 8-week government-backed Korean program that teaches solo founders to treat AI agents as co-founders across planning, development, marketing, and fundraising. Three stages: AI Base Camp (100 participants, agent design and practical application education), AI Working Group (50 teams, 1-on-1 KAIST PhD/Master's supporters plus investor business-model review), and Scale-up (25 teams, seed funding up to 150M KRW). This is one of the very few programs whose explicit mission is the agent-powered one-person company, not just AI tool adoption.
mechanics: Stage 1 – AI Base Camp: faculty-led AI agent custom design and practical application training for ~100 participants selected nationwide (50 Seoul/Gyeonggi, 50 Daejeon)
Stage 2 – AI Working Group: 1-on-1 matching with KAIST AI graduate students for technical advancement; separate business-model validation by VC/startup experts
Stage 3 – Scale-up: hackathon + pitch to select 25 teams; seed investment of up to 100M KRW per founder and commercialization funds
proof: Program launched May 18, 2026 with public information session at KAIST
Total seed pool: 2.5 billion KRW across 25 teams

candidates.name: Solo Founder Venture Sprint
candidates.summary: A 3-month in-person accelerator in San Francisco for solo founders building technology companies, run by Julian Weisser (co-founder of On Deck/ODF). Cohorts of 10 founders each receive $100K on an uncapped MFN SAFE for 2.5% equity, weekly 1:1 mentorship, and $800K+ in cloud/AI partner perks (Anthropic, OpenAI, AWS). The program does not teach an AI-agent curriculum; it provides structure, community, and funding for solo founders who happen to be building in the AI era. Distinction from Principal intent: strong on founder support, absent on teaching how to architect and run an agent-powered business.
mechanics: 10 founders per cohort, all in-person in San Francisco
Weekly 1:1 meetings with Julian Weisser on strategic priorities
Kickoff retreat, dinners with operators, office hours on fundraising and sales
$100K investment for 2.5% equity (uncapped MFN SAFE), no program fee
$800K+ in partner perks including Anthropic and OpenAI credits
proof: Alumni include Supermemory ($3M raised, 25K+ GitHub stars), Docmost (2.8M downloads)
One alumni hit $2M ARR solo in 4 months; another went to YC

candidates.name: Solo Operator AI Agent Team Build
candidates.summary: A French-language self-paced course (Solo Squad by Le Board) that teaches solo business operators to build a personal AI agent team for their business without coding. Four modules: map your ideal AI org chart, build a centralized knowledge base in Notion, build and customize 10 pre-built agent templates (sales proposals, LinkedIn content, landing pages, delegation/automation), and iterate with community feedback. It is the only French-language training found that explicitly frames the output as an operating AI team for a one-person business. It stops at operational automation and does not teach venture creation or agent architecture.
mechanics: Module 1: design your AI org chart — which agent roles does your solo business need
Module 2: centralize prompts, SOPs, and company context in Notion as agent knowledge base
Module 3: customize 10 named agent templates (sales, content, automation roles)
Module 4: community-assisted iteration and agent refinement
proof: Course is live and purchasable on leboard.fr
Created by Flavie Prévot, founder of Le Board media targeting freelancers and solopreneurs

candidates.name: AI-Enabled MVP Launch Sprint
candidates.summary: A two-week live online bootcamp from Founder Institute that teaches non-technical founders to scope, build, and launch a working MVP using AI agents and no-code tools. Participants set up an AI tool system, launch a first product to test a business hypothesis, and get mentor feedback. It sits at the earliest Principal step — idea to first-product launch — and does not cover running a multi-agent business stack or reaching revenue scale. Founder Institute has repositioned itself as an 'AI-native company builder' and explicitly says 'No Co-Founder? No Problem. AI tools and agents can get you to first traction and funding.'
mechanics: Set up an AI system of tools, integrations, and automations for the business
Use AI and no-code tools to launch a first product to test a business hypothesis
Get live feedback from mentors, experts, and fellow builders during the two weeks
Start testing the product with real customers and collecting feedback
proof: Bootcamp open for applications; Early Bird deadline was August 10, 2025
Founder Institute has run 100+ global locations and claims 9,000+ alumni companies

candidates.name: AI-First Venture Build
candidates.summary: A training Move — currently an aspiration with thin real-world evidence — that would walk a founder from idea to a revenue-positive business where AI agents handle the work of at least one department (e.g., sales, support, content, code review). Distinguished from existing accelerators by teaching agent architecture and orchestration as the core operational model, not just a productivity layer. No verified program was found that delivers this end-to-end as a curriculum in 2025-2026. The closest real approximation is KAIST OverEdge (Korean, government-funded, just launching) and Solo Squad (French, operational automation only). The gap is real and wide: every major vendor and platform (Google, Antler, Techstars, YC AI Startup School) tops out at either accelerating AI-product startups or teaching AI tool adoption — none explicitly teach the principal-level skill of designing, deploying, and managing a department-replacing agent stack as the business's operating model.
mechanics: Idea-to-agent-stack: map which business functions can be replaced by an agent, not just assisted
Agent architecture sprint: design multi-agent orchestration for sales, support, or content ops
Revenue milestone: reach first paying customer with agent stack operational
Iteration loop: replace human tasks progressively, measure cost per outcome vs. headcount
Governance: set guardrails, monitoring, and escalation paths for autonomous agent operations
proof: Real solo-founder success stories exist (Pieter Levels at $3.5M ARR solo, Ben Broca's Polsia at $1M ARR in 30 days, Maor Shlomo's Base44 acquired by Wix for $80M) but these are self-taught practitioners, not program graduates
No verified cohort-based program was found that teaches the full arc from idea to agent-run business as a structured curriculum
candidates.ecosystem: global (gap — no single ecosystem owns this)

candidates.name: Agent-Native Startup Cohort
candidates.summary: A track — currently an identified gap, not a verified program — for a multi-week cohort that explicitly teaches founding and operating a business whose core production capacity runs on an AI agent stack. No such program was found in 2025-2026. Google for Startups AI First, Antler AI Residency, and Techstars all accept and support AI-product startups, but their selection and curriculum focus on building AI products for external customers, not on teaching the founder to run their own operations via agents. The closest real offering is KAIST OverEdge, which is narrow in geography (Korea), funded by government, and just launching. A global English-language version of this track does not yet exist as a verified program.
mechanics: Week 1-2: agent-stack scoping — identify which operational functions are agent-replaceable vs. human-required
Week 3-4: build first agent (sales or support) and run it with real customers
Week 5-8: add orchestration layer, connect agent outputs, measure operational cost vs. headcount baseline
Week 9-12: revenue milestone sprint and investor readiness with agent-stack as core narrative
proof: No verified English-language cohort program found as of May 2026
KAIST OverEdge (Korean) is the only nationally-scoped program matching this definition
Antler, YC, Google for Startups, and Techstars have AI-focused tracks but teach building AI products, not running operations on agents
